should_trigger: Given only these policy sentences, build an audit-ready knowledge model with a universe of discourse, set membership rules, relation arities, first-order formulas, model assumptions, and truth-check examples.
Convert these policy sentences into controlled English templates, first-order predicates, and a model-theoretic truth check with explicit object language and metalanguage.
Our product taxonomy has multiple inheritance and attribute combinations. Build a lattice-based ontology refinement plan with the partial order, meet/join behavior, and source trace for each concept.
Turn this library database rule set into extensional and intensional relations, conceptual graph roles, quantifier scopes, and constraints that can be audited.

We have a knowledge graph with event nodes, state predicates, and n-ary relations. Decide whether to model it as tables, labeled graphs, hypergraphs, or bipartite conceptual graphs, and state what each representation preserves or loses.

Check whether the sentence 'every employee has a manager' was formalized correctly, including the difference between forall-exists and exists-forall readings, and provide a small countermodel if the formalization is too strong.
Design a controlled natural language for domain experts that maps function words and content words to logic without relying on ordinary English implicature, and reject sentences that cannot compile.
Explain why our system should not use its own unrestricted truth predicate inside the same rule language, and propose a safe object-language/metalanguage split with evaluation statuses.
A compliance rule says every shipment has a verified carrier before release. Formalize the universe, predicates, closed-world assumptions, true/false examples, and missing-reference case.
should_not_trigger: Summarize Sowa's biography in five bullet points.
Write a normal English rewrite of this paragraph with friendlier tone.
Implement a React graph visualization component without any semantic modeling requirements.
Calculate the union and intersection of two small sets for a homework exercise.
Give a high-level opinion about whether correspondence theory of truth is philosophically correct.
Draw a pretty ontology diagram for a slide deck without checking relation arity, subtype semantics, or truth conditions.
Explain what a graph is to a beginner without applying it to a knowledge model.
Proofread these controlled-English sentences for grammar only, without translating them to logic.
edge_cases: The user asks for a database schema but the rules include quantifiers, negation, and n-ary events; trigger and apply only the representation-selection, relation-arity, and constraint parts before recommending a schema.

The user only needs a glossary, but terms include subtype, equivalence class, and transitive closure; trigger a lightweight pass to prevent inconsistent formal definitions, then stop before full model construction.
The user has natural-language requirements with words like 'and' and 'the'; trigger only if those words affect parsing, reference resolution, quantifier scope, or logical commitments.
The user asks whether a claim is true but provides no universe, relation extensions, or unknown-fact policy; trigger only to ask the activation-gate questions and avoid inventing truth conditions.
The user says their category hierarchy is a lattice, but supplies only parent-child edges; trigger to verify whether meet/join operations exist and downgrade to DAG or partial order if not.
The user provides a controlled-English rule whose pronoun can refer to two prior variables; trigger to list competing parses instead of choosing silently.

must_include_for_should_trigger: universe of discourse and membership assumptions
sets, functions, relations, arities, domains, and ranges as relevant
formal or controlled-language artifact with quantifier scope
model-theoretic truth status, examples, countermodel, or evaluation rule when relevant
object-language versus metalanguage boundary when truth, syntax, or evaluation is involved
must_include_for_should_not_trigger: reason formal semantic modeling is not needed
lighter alternative such as ordinary explanation, math tutoring, or prose rewrite
must_include_for_edge_cases: blocking ambiguity or missing domain fact
smallest clarifying question or provisional assumption
downgrade path when proof/model checking is beyond the skill
must_not_do: tell the user to read the original Sowa pages before proceeding
declare truth without a specified language and model
hide relation arity or quantifier scope
pretend to be a theorem prover or model checker
